\setlength{\tabcolsep}{4pt}

\begin{tabular}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{3.0cm} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{3.2cm} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{3.2cm} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{2.8cm} >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{4.6cm}}
\hline
\textbf{Dataset} & \textbf{Study (year)} & \textbf{Model / method} & \textbf{Metric} & \textbf{Value / note}\\
\hline
\rowcolor{gray!12}\multicolumn{5}{l}{\emph{Datasets we use}}\\
\hline
Mohler & Aminah et al. (2025)~\cite{aminah_evaluating_2025} & DeepSeek & QWK / MAE & 0.902 / 0.30: open-source LLM; most balanced score distribution\\
SciEntsBank (unseen ans.) & Chen et al. (CEM-FT) (2026)~\cite{chen_cemft_2026} & CEM-FT (Qwen3-4B + LoRA) & QWK & 0.767: vs BERT 0.20 / Qwen3-8B FT 0.729; Qwen family = our roster\\
SciEntsBank & Dada et al. (iAttention) (2025)~\cite{dada_iattention_2025} & iAttention-BERT & Accuracy / Macro-F1 & 0.828 / 0.823 (2-way); 0.690 / 0.662 (3-way): inter-sentence attention; beats RoBERTa-lrg/XLNet\\
RIAYN-OOP (80) & Pathak et al. (RIAYN) (2025)~\cite{pathak_rubric_2025} & GPT-4o + question-specific rubric (CRE) & Pearson r / Cohen $\kappa$\_B / Leniency & 0.912 / 0.598 / 0.082: best on OOP; question-specific $\gg$ question-agnostic $\gg$ no-rubric\\
PT-CS (ours) & This work & -- & -- & introduced here; not previously studied as a grading benchmark\\
\hline
\rowcolor{gray!12}\multicolumn{5}{l}{\emph{Other LLM-grading studies, for context}}\\
\hline
programming assignments & Jukiewicz (2026)~\cite{jukiewicz_comparison_2026} & LLM model comparison (6000+ subs) & grade-distribution / leniency & (no QWK; distributional): 'yet another model ranking' we differentiate from\\
bioinformatics course & Policar et al. (2025)~\cite{policar_automated_2025} & LLM graders comparison & (qualitative: comparable to human) & domain-specific model comparison\\
German essays (multidim.) & Sessler et al. (Can AI grade) (2025)~\cite{sessler_canai_2025} & GPT-3.5/GPT-4/o1/LLaMA3-70B/Mixtral & Spearman r / ICC & o1 best: 0.74 / 0.80: closed$>$open; LLMs more lenient than teachers; o1 (reasoning) tops\\
algorithm/programming answers & Moazzez et al. (2024)~\cite{moazzez_assessing_2024} & 4 open-source LLMs (rubric) & ICC & Claude 0.828 (best) / Copilot 0.293 (worst): model choice dominates consistency\\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table*}
